conditions: Post-accident orthopedic injuries (pelvic fracture healing, right knee ligament sprain)
health.mental_health: Recovery has been emotionally rough: she has anxiety around traffic and being a passenger, frustration about slower mobility, and periods of low mood when pain and migraines flare; she is doing brief CBT-based counseling and uses grounding techniques during PT.
recent_visits: Orthopedic follow-up (imaging to confirm fracture healing, cleared for increased weight-bearing)
Physical therapy (2x/week) focused on gait retraining and knee stability
Neurology consult for migraine/post-concussion symptom management
OB-GYN visit to review PCOS labs and adjust metformin dose
Pain management consult to avoid over-reliance on NSAIDs

finance.debt: Ongoing medical bills from the accident (ER, imaging, orthopedic surgery consults, PT) on a payment plan; she also has a car loan and has used a 0% APR balance transfer to cover out-of-pocket costs while she rebuilds her emergency fund.
recent_transactions: Physical therapy copays and deductible payments
Neurology appointment and migraine medication refill
Ride-share and parking costs for medical visits (reduced driving since the accident)
Replacement ergonomic chair and split keyboard for home office setup
Insurance premium increase and auto claim deductible payment

relationships: Partner: Maya Alvarez (helps with driving to appointments and meal prep during flare-ups)
Best friend: Priya Natarajan (checks in after PT sessions; helps her stay social without overdoing it)
Older brother: Kevin Liu (in Houston; calls weekly and handled some insurance paperwork right after the accident)
PT buddy: Jordan McCall (met at the clinic; occasional coffee after sessions when she’s up for it)
social.social_media_activity: Mostly lurks on Instagram and Reddit; posts occasional Austin food/coffee photos and a few recovery milestones without details. Keeps accident specifics off public feeds and uses close-friends stories for health updates.
private_conversations: Discussing fertility concerns and irregular cycles related to PCOS with her partner
Talking about fear of driving and panic spikes near the intersection where the accident happened
Negotiating boundaries at work around on-call rotations while she’s in PT and dealing with migraines
Sharing frustrations about weight changes and acne/hair growth tied to PCOS treatment

schedule.work_hours: Typically Mon–Fri 9:30am–6:00pm with one on-call week every 6–8 weeks; currently has a temporary accommodation to avoid late-night incident response except for high-severity events.
regular_appointments: Physical therapy: Tuesdays and Thursdays at 7:30am (Austin Sports Medicine & PT near North Loop)
upcoming_events: Orthopedic re-evaluation and strength testing next Monday morning
Work tabletop incident-response exercise (half-day) in two weeks
Friend’s birthday dinner on South Congress (planning to arrive early to avoid standing too long)
PCOS lab draw scheduled for next month (fasting)
schedule.daily_routine: Wakes around 6:30am on PT days for stretching/ice, works mostly from home, takes short walking breaks per PT plan, avoids long meetings back-to-back due to migraines, and keeps evenings low-key with heat therapy, meal prep, and early sleep when symptoms spike.

professional.company: Mid-size Austin-based fintech (payments and fraud prevention) with a hybrid workforce and 24/7 security operations
projects: Tuning SIEM detections and reducing false positives for cloud workloads (AWS + Azure)
Implementing phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2) for privileged accounts
Incident response playbook updates for ransomware and data exfiltration scenarios
Quarterly access review automation with IAM team
Vendor security assessment for a new customer-support SaaS tool
professional.performance_issues: Generally strong performer, but recovery has caused intermittent missed early-morning standups and reduced availability during migraine/post-concussion flare-ups; she has an HR-documented temporary accommodation and is careful about not disclosing more health detail than necessary to her broader team.
workplace_relationships: Manager: Elena Park (supportive, helps shield her from nonessential on-call tasks during PT-heavy weeks)
SOC lead: Marcus Delaney (expects quick responses; occasional tension when Zainab logs off for appointments)
Close teammate: Samir Qureshi (covers alerts during her PT sessions; she reciprocates by handling more documentation)
IT director: Rachel Nguyen (pushes for faster rollout of MFA; Zainab is the technical point person)

hobbies: Home espresso and trying new coffee shops around Austin
Low-impact yoga and mobility work (adapted from PT)
Casual gaming (co-op games with friends when she can’t go out)
Indoor climbing (paused during recovery; hopes to return when cleared)
habits: Uses a migraine trigger log (sleep, hydration, screen time, weather changes)
Wears blue-light filters and takes scheduled screen breaks to prevent headaches
Uses a cane on bad knee days and avoids long walks in heat
Heat/ice therapy nightly and compression sleeve for the knee during longer sitting
Limits alcohol because it worsens migraines and disrupts sleep
Keeps a small emergency medication kit in her bag (sumatriptan, ondansetron, NSAIDs)
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives in a second-floor apartment in North Austin with her partner; they rearranged furniture for clearer pathways and set up a dedicated home office so she can work comfortably on higher-pain days. She’s avoiding stairs when possible and plans errands around elevator access and parking proximity.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: High-protein, lower-glycemic meals to support PCOS and energy; tries to meal-prep simple lunches, stays hydrated for migraine prevention, and keeps caffeine consistent (one espresso in the morning, avoids afternoon caffeine).
